scope: kolm distillation + serving platform (managed cloud) and self-hosted distribution (BYOC).

controls.question: Are applications and programming interfaces (APIs) designed, developed, deployed, and tested in accordance with industry standards (e.g., OWASP)?
controls.evidence: OWASP ASVS L2 controls applied across HTTP surface. All public-API routes pass through src/auth.js (auth) + src/route-table.js (input shape) + src/audit-static-refs.cjs (no dead refs). CI fails on any route without an auth gate or input validator.

controls.question: Are data input and output integrity routines (i.e., MD5 / SHA checksums) implemented for application interfaces and databases?
controls.evidence: Every .kolm artifact ships with a four-ring receipt chain (data, train, eval, sigstore) anchored by ed25519 signatures + SHA-256 manifests. `kolm verify` replays the chain offline. Source: src/sigstore.js, src/receipt.js.

controls.question: Is there a documented business continuity / disaster recovery (BCP / DR) plan?
controls.evidence: Managed cloud runs on Vercel (web tier) + Railway (API tier) with daily KOLM_DATA_DIR snapshots to S3-compatible object storage. RTO 4h, RPO 24h. Self-hosted customers retain full sovereignty — see docs/self-hosted-deploy-complete.md.

controls.question: Does the organization maintain a tested incident response plan?
controls.evidence: Pagerduty + Statuspage wired. Sev-1 response: 15min ack, 1h initial comms, post-mortem within 5 business days published at /trust/postmortems. Incident playbooks at docs/runbooks/.

controls.question: Are policies and procedures in place to govern changes to the production environment?
controls.evidence: All production deploys go through git → CI (scripts/release-verify.cjs 7 gates) → human review → tagged release. No direct prod console access; root credentials in HashiCorp Vault, broken-glass procedure requires two-person rule.

controls.question: Are cryptographic protections deployed for data at rest?
controls.evidence: All persistent data encrypted at rest via storage-provider-managed AES-256 (Vercel, Railway, S3). Tenant API keys hashed with argon2id at the database boundary.

controls.question: Are cryptographic protections deployed for data in transit?
controls.evidence: TLS 1.2+ enforced on all public surfaces (HSTS preload). Internal services mTLS via Tailscale. No plaintext HTTP outside of localhost dev.

controls.question: Are cryptographic keys rotated periodically?
controls.evidence: Sigstore ring keys rotated every 90 days via scripts/rotate-sigstore-keys.cjs. Tenant API keys are user-rotatable from /account/keys with one-click revoke. TLS certs auto-rotated by hosting provider (60d).

controls.question: Are physical security perimeters implemented at the datacenter?
controls.evidence: Managed cloud relies on SOC2-certified hosting providers (Vercel — AWS us-east, Railway — GCP us-central). Physical security inherited from underlying provider; SOC2 reports available under NDA via security@kolm.ai.

controls.question: Is sensitive customer data identified, classified, and labeled?
controls.evidence: Customer data classified as Tenant-Private (default), Tenant-Public (opt-in), or Anonymous-Aggregate. PII redaction default-on via src/redact.js + workers/media-redact. Classification is per-namespace and enforced at the event-store boundary.

controls.question: Does the organization comply with GDPR / CCPA / equivalent regional privacy laws?
controls.evidence: DPA template at /legal/dpa, sub-processor list at /legal/subprocessors, data-subject-request flow at /privacy/request. Self-hosted (BYOC) tenants run in their own cloud — no data egress; Kolm is not a processor.

controls.question: Is there a documented data retention and deletion policy?
controls.evidence: Tenant data retained 90 days after subscription end; deletion-on-request honored within 30 days (GDPR Art 17). Receipts (cryptographic proofs) retained indefinitely as anchored hashes only (no raw data).

controls.question: Is there a documented information security management system (ISMS)?
controls.evidence: ISMS aligned with ISO/IEC 27001 controls. SOC 2 Type II in progress (audit period 2026-01-01 to 2026-12-31, report Q1 2027). Type I report available now under NDA.

controls.question: Do personnel undergo background checks prior to employment?
controls.evidence: All employees and contractors with production access pass third-party background check (Checkr) prior to onboarding. Re-checks every 24 months for those with persistent prod credentials.

controls.question: Is multi-factor authentication (MFA) enforced for privileged users?
controls.evidence: MFA mandatory for all employees with prod access (TOTP + WebAuthn). Customer-facing SSO via SAML 2.0 + SCIM supports IdP-enforced MFA (Okta, Azure AD, Google Workspace) on Enterprise and Business tiers.

controls.question: Is access provisioned based on least privilege and need-to-know?
controls.evidence: Role-based access control (RBAC) with four roles (owner, admin, member, viewer). Default to viewer; promotion requires owner approval. Audit log at /account/audit captures every role mutation.

controls.question: Can customer data and configurations be exported in standard formats?
controls.evidence: Full tenant export via `kolm export-tenant --out tenant.tar.gz` (CLI) or `/v1/tenant/export` (API). Includes specs, artifacts (.kolm), receipts, captures (jsonl), and audit log. No proprietary lock-in.

controls.question: Is network traffic between tenants isolated?
controls.evidence: Multi-tenancy enforced at the application layer via tenant_id-scoped event-store reads/writes (src/event-store.js findByTenant). Defense-in-depth: per-row tenant_id checks inside every loop. BYOC customers get hard physical isolation.

controls.question: Are firewalls or equivalent network controls deployed?
controls.evidence: Vercel + Railway provide DDoS-mitigated edge. Application-layer rate limits at src/rate-limit.js (per-IP + per-tenant + per-route). Public endpoints PUBLIC_API explicitly allow-listed in src/auth.js.

controls.question: Are security-relevant events logged and monitored?
controls.evidence: Auth events (login, logout, key issue, key revoke, role change) logged to the tenant audit trail. Public access logs retained 30d. Anomaly detection on failed-auth rate; alerts to security@kolm.ai.

controls.question: Are customers notified of security incidents that may affect them?
controls.evidence: Customer notification within 72h of confirmed breach (GDPR Art 33). Postmortems published at /trust/postmortems. Status page at status.kolm.ai for service-level incidents.

controls.question: Is there a vendor risk management program?
controls.evidence: Sub-processors listed at /legal/subprocessors. Each sub-processor reviewed annually for SOC 2 + DPA + data residency. List of evaluated AI inference providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Modal, RunPod) at /trust/sub-processors.

controls.question: Are vulnerabilities identified and remediated on a defined schedule?
controls.evidence: npm audit + pip-audit + cargo audit run in CI. SLAs: critical 7d, high 30d, medium 90d. Penetration test annually by independent third party; latest report Q2 2026 (NDA).

controls.question: Is there a coordinated vulnerability disclosure (CVD) program?
controls.evidence: security.txt at /.well-known/security.txt. Safe-harbor language at /security/disclosure. PGP key published. 90-day disclosure timeline; bounty program at hackerone.com/kolm (Q3 2026).

controls.question: Are endpoints (laptops, servers) hardened and managed?
controls.evidence: Employee laptops MDM-enrolled (Kandji). Full-disk encryption, OS patches within 14d, no local admin, password manager mandatory (1Password), USB write-block.

notes: This is a curated subset of CAIQ v4.0.2 controls covering the highest-asked enterprise procurement questions. Full 261-control workbook available via `kolm procurement caiq --full --out caiq.xlsx` or by request to security@kolm.ai.
